content_en:
Ahmad ibn Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, known as Ibn Hajar, was born in Cairo in 773 AH (1372 CE). He is universally regarded as the greatest hadith scholar of the post-classical period and one of the towering figures in the entire history of Islamic scholarship. His life spanned decades of intense scholarly activity, during which he produced works of extraordinary scope, depth, and influence in hadith criticism, biographical literature, Qur'anic commentary, and jurisprudence.

Ibn Hajar's education was comprehensive and distinguished. He studied under the leading scholars of Egypt and Syria, traveled extensively in the Hijaz and other regions to study hadith, and eventually emerged as the acknowledged master of the hadith sciences in the Arabic-speaking world. His flagship work, Fath al-Bari — the great commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari — took him over twenty years to complete and remains the standard reference for understanding al-Bukhari's collection.

Beyond his scholarly work, Ibn Hajar served as chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of the Shafi'i school in Egypt on multiple occasions during the Mamluk sultanate — a position of great prestige and institutional significance. His combination of unparalleled hadith scholarship and legal authority made him the preeminent religious figure of his era in Egypt.

The Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah — "Reaching the Target in Distinguishing the Companions" — represents the culmination of the literature on Companions of the Prophet. Ibn Hajar drew on all earlier works dealing with the Sahabah — including Ibn Abd al-Barr's Isti'ab, Ibn al-Athir's Usd al-Ghabah, and numerous other sources — and synthesized them into the most comprehensive treatment of this subject ever produced. The Mamluk period context provided both the institutional support for such a massive project and the scholarly infrastructure of major libraries and learned colleagues that made it possible. Ibn Hajar died in Cairo in 852 AH (1449 CE).
content_id:
Ahmad bin Ali bin Muhammad bin Muhammad bin Hajar al-Asqalani, yang dikenal sebagai Ibnu Hajar, lahir di Kairo pada 773 H (1372 M). Ia secara universal dianggap sebagai ulama hadis terbesar pada periode pasca-klasik dan salah satu tokoh terkemuka dalam seluruh sejarah keilmuan Islam. Hidupnya mencakup dekade-dekade aktivitas keilmuan yang intens, di mana ia menghasilkan karya-karya dengan cakupan, kedalaman, dan pengaruh yang luar biasa dalam kritik hadis, literatur biografi, tafsir Al-Qur'an, dan fikih.

Pendidikan Ibnu Hajar sangat komprehensif dan terhormat. Ia belajar kepada para ulama terkemuka Mesir dan Suriah, melakukan perjalanan yang ekstensif ke Hijaz dan wilayah-wilayah lain untuk mempelajari hadis, dan akhirnya muncul sebagai master yang diakui dari ilmu hadis di dunia berbahasa Arab. Karya utamanya, Fath al-Bari — syarh agung atas Shahih al-Bukhari — membutuhkan waktu lebih dari dua puluh tahun untuk diselesaikan dan tetap menjadi referensi standar untuk memahami koleksi al-Bukhari.

Di luar karya ilmiahnya, Ibnu Hajar menjabat sebagai hakim agung (qadi al-qudat) mazhab Syafi'i di Mesir beberapa kali selama kesultanan Mamluk — sebuah posisi dengan prestise besar dan makna institusional. Kombinasinya antara ilmu hadis yang tak tertandingi dan otoritas hukum menjadikannya tokoh agama paling terkemuka di eranya di Mesir.

Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah — 'Mencapai Sasaran dalam Membedakan Para Sahabat' — merepresentasikan puncak dari literatur tentang para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ. Ibnu Hajar menggunakan semua karya sebelumnya yang membahas Sahabah — termasuk Al-Isti'ab karya Ibnu Abd al-Barr, Usd al-Ghabah karya Ibnu al-Atsir, dan banyak sumber lainnya — dan mensintesisnya menjadi perlakuan paling komprehensif tentang subjek ini yang pernah diproduksi. Ibnu Hajar meninggal di Kairo pada 852 H (1449 M).
content_ar: أحمد ibn علي ibn محمد ﷺ ibn محمد ﷺ ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, known as Ibn Hajar, was born in Cairo in 773 AH (1372 CE). He is universally regarded as the greatest الحديث scholar of the post-الكلاسيكي period and one of the towering figures in the entire history of Islamic scholarship. His الحياة spanned decades of intense العلمي activity, خلال which he produced works of extraordinary scope, depth, and influence in الحديث criticism, biographical literature, Qur'anic commentary, and jurisprudence. Ibn Hajar's education was comprehensive and distinguished. He studied under the leading العلماء of Egypt and Syria, traveled extensively in the Hijaz and other regions to study الحديث, and eventually emerged as the acknowledged master of the الحديث sciences in the Arabic-speaking الدنيا. His flagship work, Fath al-Bari — the great commentary on صحيح البخاري — took him over twenty years to complete and remains the standard reference for الفهم البخاري's collection. Beyond his العلمي work, Ibn Hajar served as chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of the الشافعي school in Egypt on multiple occasions خلال the Mamluk sultanate — a position of great prestige and institutional significance. His combination of unparalleled الحديث scholarship and legal authority made him the preeminent religious figure of his era in Egypt. The Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-الصحابة — "Reaching the Target in Distinguishing the الصحابة" — represents the culmination of the literature on الصحابة of النبي ﷺ. Ibn Hajar drew on all earlier works dealing with the الصحابة — بما في ذلك Ibn Abd al-Barr's Isti'ab, Ibn al-Athir's Usd al-Ghabah, and numerous other sources — and synthesized them into the most comprehensive treatment of this subject ever produced. The Mamluk period context provided both the institutional support for such a massive project and the العلمي infrastructure of major libraries and learned colleagues that made it possible. Ibn Hajar died in Cairo in 852 AH (1449 CE).
